key_insight: The paper shows that flat-band materials can be designed by treating band flatness as a connectivity problem and generating novel sublattice geometries beyond the usual kagome/Lieb/pyrochlore motifs.
engineering_relevance: This expands the materials design search space for discovering compounds with correlated and topological electronic behavior using symmetry-aware generative modeling.
method: Build a continuous geometric representation of crystal sublattices to explore non-catalog crystal-net motifs.
Use SkeleGen to place unconventional skeletons on symmetry-compatible Wyckoff positions while denoising surrounding chemistry.
Screen generated candidates for stability and flat-band behavior with high-throughput DFT and tight-binding checks.
evidence_signal: The workflow produced 9,352 crystal candidates that passed stability and flatness screening, with DFT band structures agreeing well with tight-binding spectra of the isolated skeletons.
watch_item: Generative novelty does not guarantee experimental synthesizability, so the unusual motifs still need practical validation beyond computational screening.
paper_title: Symmetry-aware generative design of flat-band materials beyond known crystal-net prototypes
